text: We describe the case of a relaparoscopic approach of recurrence after laparoscopic incisional hernia repair. We would like to demonstrate the feasibility and benefits of the relaparoscopic approach of a recurrent incisional hernia originally treated by laparoscopy. This is a case of a sixty-five-year-old male obese patient with history of multiple laparotomies who underwent laparoscopic repair of a paramedial hypogastric incisional hernia. During surgery, dissection of staph additions and enterolysis were done.
The surgeon performs primary closure of the abdominal wall defect following the resection of the hernia sac, preparing for the placement of a dual mesh.

During the laparoscopic re-operation, the surgeon dissects multiple adhesions to expose the hernia sac. A three-centimeter defect at the upper edge of the PTFE dual mesh is identified and closed with non-absorbable stitches, securing the mesh to the fascial margin.
The surgeon places a larger intraperitoneal dual mesh prosthesis to cover the entire abdominal wall, ensuring complete coverage of the repaired area.
